info.name: Microsoft Azure Storage Cache Mgmt Client
info.description: A Storage Cache provides scalable caching service for NAS clients, serving data from either NFSv3 or Blob at-rest storage (referred to as "Storage Targets"). These operations allow you to manage Caches.

item.name: Microsoft Azure Get Providers Microsoft Storagecache Operations

request.description: Lists all of the available Resource Provider operations.

response.name: The list of available Resource Provider operations.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

response.name: Error response describing why the operation failed.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

request.description: Get the list of StorageCache.Cache SKUs available to this subscription.

response.name: A list of SKU descriptors.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

response.name: Error response describing why the operation failed.

item.name: Microsoft Azure Get Subscriptions Subscriptionid Providers Microsoft Storagecache Caches

request.description: Returns all caches the user has access to under a subscription.

response.name: A list of cache objects. Note that entity references might replace complete cache objects, as described in http://docs.oasis-open.org/odata/odata-json-format/v4.01/cs01/odata-json-format-v4.01-cs01.html#sec_EntityReference

item.name: Microsoft Azure Post Subscriptions Subscriptionid Resourcegroups Resourcegroupname Providers Microsoft Storagecache Caches Cachename Storagetargets Storagetargetname Dnsrefresh

variable.description: (Required) Name of cache. Length of name must not be greater than 80 and chars must be from the [-0-9a-zA-Z_] char class.
(Required) Name of Storage Target.

request.description: Tells a storage target to refresh its DNS information.

response.name: Cache has refreshed the storage target's DNS information.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

response.name: Cache has started refreshing storage target DNS information.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

item.name: Microsoft Azure Post Subscriptions Subscriptionid Resourcegroups Resourcegroupname Providers Microsoft Storagecache Caches Cachename Storagetargets Storagetargetname Flush

variable.description: (Required) Name of cache. Length of name must not be greater than 80 and chars must be from the [-0-9a-zA-Z_] char class.
(Required) Name of Storage Target.

request.description: Tells the cache to write all dirty data to the Storage Target's backend storage. Client requests to this storage target's namespace will return errors until the flush operation completes.

response.name: All data has been flushed to the storage target.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

response.name: Cache has started flushing to the storage target. Poll the storage target's state field to monitor.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

description.content: Location URI to poll for result
URI to poll for the operation status

response.name: Flush already in progress or has completed.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

response.name: Error response describing why the operation failed.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

request.description: Suspends client access to a storage target.

response.name: Client access to the storage target has been suspended.

response.name: Storage target suspend has started. Poll the storage target's state field to monitor.

request.description: Resumes client access to a previously suspended storage target.

response.name: Client access to the storage target has been resumed.

response.name: Storage target resume has started. Poll the storage target's state field to monitor.

item.name: Microsoft Azure Post Subscriptions Subscriptionid Resourcegroups Resourcegroupname Providers Microsoft Storagecache Caches Cachename Storagetargets Storagetargetname Invalidate

variable.description: (Required) Name of cache. Length of name must not be greater than 80 and chars must be from the [-0-9a-zA-Z_] char class.
(Required) Name of Storage Target.

request.description: Invalidate all cached data for a storage target. Cached files are discarded and fetched from the back end on the next request.

response.name: All cached data for the storage target was invalidated

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

response.name: Storage target invalidation has started. Poll the storage target's state field to monitor progress.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

description.content: Location URI to poll for result
URI to poll for the operation status

response.name: The storage target already has been invalidated, or invalidate is in progress.

response.name: Error response describing why the operation failed.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

item.name: Microsoft Azure Delete Subscriptions Subscriptionid Resourcegroups Resourcegroupname Providers Microsoft Storagecache Caches Cachename Storagetargets Storagetargetname

query.description: Boolean value requesting the force delete operation for a storage target. Force delete discards unwritten-data in the cache instead of flushing it to back-end storage.

variable.description: (Required) Name of cache. Length of name must not be greater than 80 and chars must be from the [-0-9a-zA-Z_] char class.
(Required) Name of Storage Target.

request.description: Removes a Storage Target from a cache. This operation is allowed at any time, but if the cache is down or unhealthy, the actual removal of the Storage Target may be delayed until the cache is healthy again. Note that if the cache has data to flush to the Storage Target, the data will be flushed before the Storage Target will be deleted.

response.name: Storage Target deleted.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

query.description: Boolean value requesting the force delete operation for a storage target. Force delete discards unwritten-data in the cache instead of flushing it to back-end storage.

response.name: Started the Storage Target's deletion. Poll the cache's Storage Targets to monitor.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

query.description: Boolean value requesting the force delete operation for a storage target. Force delete discards unwritten-data in the cache instead of flushing it to back-end storage.

description.content: Location URI to poll for result
URI to poll for the operation status

response.name: Storage Target deleted.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

query.description: Boolean value requesting the force delete operation for a storage target. Force delete discards unwritten-data in the cache instead of flushing it to back-end storage.

response.name: Error response describing why the operation failed.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

query.description: Boolean value requesting the force delete operation for a storage target. Force delete discards unwritten-data in the cache instead of flushing it to back-end storage.

item.name: Microsoft Azure Put Subscriptions Subscriptionid Resourcegroups Resourcegroupname Providers Microsoft Storagecache Caches Cachename Storagetargets Storagetargetname

variable.description: (Required) Name of cache. Length of name must not be greater than 80 and chars must be from the [-0-9a-zA-Z_] char class.
(Required) Name of Storage Target.

request.description: Create or update a Storage Target. This operation is allowed at any time, but if the cache is down or unhealthy, the actual creation/modification of the Storage Target may be delayed until the cache is healthy again.

response.name: Storage Target has been created or updated.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

response.name: Storage Target creation or update has been initiated. Poll the Storage Target's provisioningState property to monitor creation/update progress.

request.description: Tells a cache to write all dirty data to the Storage Target(s). During the flush, clients will see errors returned until the flush is complete.

response.name: All cached data has been flushed to the Storage Target(s).
